behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary: CSV parser must preserve row index, validator must reject, and DLQ writer must carry the same row number. A Unit test of validator logic alone would miss handoff/contract bugs. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require real S3/network/OS effects; this can be caught with in-process wiring and a local/fake DLQ sink.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline components together on a small CSV fixture with missing required fields; assert rejected records are written to DLQ payloads containing exact original CSV row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is function-level normalization logic (input date string -> canonical date). No stated cross-module handoff, real infra, or model output dependency. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is strictly about transformation correctness, not parser/DB/DLQ interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of the date-normalization function with ISO 8601, US, and European examples (plus invalid/ambiguous cases), asserting exact canonical output.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This is a runtime resource/timeout failure boundary (memory pressure, wall-clock execution), which depends on real process behavior and cannot be trusted with pure in-process mocks. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior explicitly requires timeout and memory guarantees under large load.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a 500k-row fixture, enforce configured timeout, and assert successful completion plus memory usage stays below threshold (no OOM/restart).

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references real S3 503 response handling. Real HTTP/status-to-error mapping and retry timing can be hidden by mocks, so MOCK-HIDE pushes above Integration. Integration was considered but rejected because the disqualifying text is "S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error," which is an external HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3-compatible endpoint/test bucket that returns a real transient 503 then succeeds; assert exponential backoff intervals and that batch processing continues to completion.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff/idempotency across pipeline stages and DB writes. A Unit test would miss transaction/checkpoint interaction. System was considered but rejected because behavior can be reproduced by re-instantiating pipeline components with persistent local PostgreSQL state; no required real network/OS primitive is stated beyond local DB semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local PostgreSQL test DB, process part of a batch, simulate restart by creating a new pipeline instance from persisted checkpoint, resume, and assert no duplicate rows (e.g., by unique keys/count checks).
